place: Moonachie borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Moonachie takes its name from the 1600s Iroquois chief Monaghie, who once watched the cedar forests here.
During WWII, five Moonachie boys lost their lives, and the borough later honored them by naming streets after each.
In 1947, Moonachie established its first ambulance corps and welcomed mobile homes, marking a postwar shift in community life.
The 1950s saw Moonachie flourish with new civic groups, including the Panoramic Civic Association and Girl Scout troops formed in 1957.
A century ago, 103 property owners were dispossessed, leaving homes abandoned and farms barren, reshaping the borough’s landscape.
Roads were upgraded, parks added, and the first municipal building opened on Moonachie Road in 1954, boosting local infrastructure.
